title: Monolophus wongsuwaniae
intro: Monolophus wongsuwaniae is a species of plant in the family Menispermaceae, commonly known as the Thai arrowroot or Thai snail vine. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Monolophus wongsuwaniae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Thai medicine, Monolophus wongsuwaniae is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Monolophus wongsuwaniae is a genus of extinct conodonts, and its species, Monolophus wongsuwaniae, falls under the phylum Chordata. It belongs to the class Conodonta, and its order is Prioniodida. The family of this species is Prioniodinidae, and it is characterized by its monolophous conical tooth structure. The classification of Monolophus wongsuwaniae is significant in understanding the evolution of conodonts during the Early Ordovician period. Further research is needed to confirm its exact position within the conodont classification.
